### Post by redDEADresolve on 2006-12-21
I'm running Edgy with an ATI X1600 Pro. I have the proprietary driver installed and everything works great, if anything XGL was been stable for once. The problem is that the resolution for the login screen is wonky. Seems to be 1920x1080. My monitor resolution is 1280x1024. I've tried some howto's but nothing seems to apply/work.

Any help?

```

# /etc/X11/xorg.conf (xorg X Window System server configuration file)
#
# This file was generated by dexconf, the Debian X Configuration tool, using
# values from the debconf database.
#
# Edit this file with caution, and see the /etc/X11/xorg.conf manual page.
# (Type "man /etc/X11/xorg.conf" at the shell prompt.)
#
# This file is automatically updated on xserver-xorg package upgrades *only*
# if it has not been modified since the last upgrade of the xserver-xorg
# package.
#
# If you have edited this file but would like it to be automatically updated
# again, run the following command:
#   sudo dpkg-reconfigure -phigh xserver-xorg

Section "ServerLayout"
	Identifier     "Default Layout"
	Screen      0  "aticonfig-Screen[0]" 0 0
	InputDevice    "Generic Keyboard"
	InputDevice    "Configured Mouse"
	InputDevice    "stylus" "SendCoreEvents"
	InputDevice    "cursor" "SendCoreEvents"

EndSection

Section "Files"

	# path to defoma fonts
	FontPath     "/usr/share/X11/fonts/misc"

	FontPath     "/usr/share/X11/fonts/Type1"
	FontPath     "/usr/share/X11/fonts/100dpi"
	FontPath     "/usr/share/X11/fonts/75dpi"
	FontPath     "/usr/share/fonts/X11/misc"

EndSection

Section "Module"
	Load  "i2c"
	Load  "bitmap"

	Load  "dri"
	Load  "extmod"
	Load  "freetype"
	Load  "glx"

	Load  "vbe"
EndSection

Section "InputDevice"
	Identifier  "Generic Keyboard"
	Driver      "kbd"
	Option	    "CoreKeyboard"
	Option	    "XkbRules" "xorg"
	Option	    "XkbModel" "pc105"
	Option	    "XkbLayout" "us"
	Option	    "XkbOptions" "lv3:ralt_switch"
EndSection

Section "InputDevice"
	Identifier  "Configured Mouse"
	Driver      "mouse"
	Option	    "CorePointer"
	Option	    "Device" "/dev/input/mice"
	Option	    "Protocol" "ExplorerPS/2"
	Option	    "ZAxisMapping" "4 5"
	Option	    "Emulate3Buttons" "true"
EndSection

Section "InputDevice"

                                                      # for USB
	Identifier  "stylus"
	Driver      "wacom"
	Option	    "Device" "/dev/wacom"          # Change to

EndSection

	Driver      "wacom"

	Option	    "ForceDevice" "ISDV4"               # Tablet PC ONLY
EndSection

Section "Monitor"
	Identifier   "VA912-4SERIE"
	Option	    "DPMS"
EndSection

Section "Monitor"
	Identifier   "aticonfig-Monitor[0]"
	Option	    "VendorName" "ATI Proprietary Driver"
	Option	    "ModelName" "Generic Autodetecting Monitor"
	Option	    "DPMS" "true"
EndSection

Section "Device"
	Identifier  "ATI Technologies, Inc. ATI Default Card"
	Driver      "vesa"
	BusID       "PCI:1:0:0"
EndSection

Section "Device"

	Monitor    "VA912-4SERIE"
```

### Post by darrenm on 2006-12-21
Hello,

Can you try sudo aticonfig --initial --force then sudo aticonfig -ovt=Xv

Thanks :)

### Post by darrenm on 2006-12-22
> **redDEADresolve said:**
> damn me this problem is so retarded my problem is exactly like others but nothing is working for me. when i go to system>preferences>screen resolution I get these resolutions

1920x1080
1777x1000
1280x1024 - the resolution of my monitor
1280x720
1152x864
1024x768
800x600
640x480

But if you look above to xorg.file these aren't listed. WTF any help?

Sorry revising my suggestion:
```
sudo mv /etc/X11/xorg.conf /etc/X11/xorg.conf.backup
sudo /etc/init.d/gdm stop
(login on terminal)
sudo dpkg-reconfigure xserver-xorg
(run through)
sudo aticonfig --initial
sudo aticonfig --ovt=Xv

```
It looks like your xorg.conf has some unwanted stuff in there.

### Post by Christopher on 2006-12-27
Im having the same problem with my login screen resolution also, my desktop is 1280x1024 85Hz. But my login screen isnt using that resolution for some reason. Does anyone know what i can do to fix it? Im using the Nvidia drivers from Alberto Milones script  [http://albertomilone.com/nvidia_scripts1.html](http://albertomilone.com/nvidia_scripts1.html)  But other then my login screen everything is great. Im using a GeForce 3 Ti200 for my video card. :confused:

### Post by Christopher on 2006-12-28
Can anyone help me with this problem? Thank you in advance.

---

### Post by Christopher on 2006-12-29
My problem was solved by doing this sudo gedit /etc/X11/xorg.conf, then removing 1600x1200 which was the 1st option, now my 1st option is 1280x1024 and the login screen looks good. Hope this helps some else out.

---
